programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The body of a politician is found in an empty house, leading the team to investigate whether he was killed as a consequence of his corrupt policies that led to local residents being forced out of their homes. Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy manages to foil a home invasion, but when Gibbs is called in to investigate, he suspects the teenager may be withholding crucial information. As the case makes progress, Gibbs turns his attention to helping the boy's mother, the wife of a naval officer, and his efforts result in an invitation to the White House to meet the First Lady (Michelle Obama, guest starring as herself). Meanwhile, Director Vance and FBI Agent Fornell travel to London as part of the ongoing case involving the escaped British spies

programme.title: The Fugitive

Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Danny and Lindsay investigate the death of a doll-shop owner found buried beneath his dismantled stock. Meanwhile, Stella and Dr Hawkes learn a waitress has been killed in a new flat given to her by a customer. Detective drama, with Gary Sinise

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A roller derby star is killed during a game, but it is unclear which side the culprit belongs to. Meanwhile, an author is found dead with the final chapter of his novel scrawled all over his body, leading the detectives to conclude he may have suffered from a compulsive urge to write. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter, Cabe and Happy get trapped aboard a top-secret submarine stuck at the bottom of the ocean, and battle to make it out alive before the self-destruct function activates

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. An indentation on the victim's skull leads the agents to believe he was killed on a British ship, but collecting evidence from the vessel proves challenging. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.desc: The former leader of an Irish Republican group comes forward with information regarding the abduction and subsequent disappearance of an 18-year-old woman in 1983, prompting the team to reopen the case. The reformed dissident claims the rebels used the kidnapping to raise their profile, but his subsequent reinvention as a politician and man of peace leaves Sandra and her colleagues suspicious of his motives. They also investigate whether the arms-manufacturing activities of the victim's father could have made her a target. Crime drama, guest starring Sian Phillips and Annette Crosbie, with Amanda Redman and James Bolam

Drama, starring Leslie Ash and Philip Middlemiss

Military adventure, starring Sean Bean and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Thornley Manor turns out to be a listed building and the lads are forced to down tools while negotiations take place at a higher level. During the unexpected interlude, they set about making themselves unpopular with local pub landlord Arthur Pringle. Tim Healy and Jimmy Nail star, with Bryan Pringle

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to investigate why newly planted roses at an upmarket preparatory school keep dying. After witnessing the arcane ritual of the Night of the Stag, they detect tensions between modernising headmaster Quentin Marshall and established members of his staff who are harbouring secrets someone is prepared to kill for. Drama, guest starring Anthony Andrews (Brideshead Revisited), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered sleuths are hired to work behind the scenes of a garden makeover TV show, but Rosemary has an accident while standing in for the programme's star and ends up in hospital. programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The coffin of local poet Declan O'Grady falls from the hearse revealing that the corpse inside is not his but a young woman identifiable only by a notable scarlet tattoo. Funeral director Warren Bugle directs Mike, Kristin and Breen to Wadsworth Manor where a mysterious role-playing game was conducted the night before Declan's death.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov is shot dead during an apparent burglary, Neville is quick to conclude that the scene was staged and Pasha was murdered. But the four people under suspicion - the victim's family and domestic workers - all have seemingly solid alibis. However, matters are complicated when the discovery of new evidence links the crime to a case from 20 years ago. First of a two-part story, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a semi in Birmingham, a plot of land in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, and a grand plan for three flats in Lewisham, south-east London

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Fabio and Isobel Avemaria think living by the sea is just what their young family needs after health scares and redundancy. They consider whether now is the right time to make a move to Norfolk. Presented by Chris Bavin

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reveals how a gang of international bag thieves were brought down to earth by an aviation crime squad. Plus, thefts from an elderly woman - were her carers or her kin to blame?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles looks at surgical equipment from the past, courtesy of the Bath Medical Museum

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Balearic crime-fighting duo Max Winter and Miranda Blake are sent to marshlands at a remote part of the island to search for a missing diabetic British birdwatcher. They soon find themselves in a race against time after uncovering an international bird smuggling ring. Julian Looman and Elen Rhys star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by heading to Thailand, where they discover why Bangkok has a reputation as the street food capital of the world, and help serve up school dinners. Heading out into the country's central plains, the duo join paddy-field workers and find out how rice is grown

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a woman who wants to move to rural Devon on a £450,000 budget, and also heads to the seaside town of Dawlish to join in with the carnival

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families promote their speciality cheeses and different forms of garlic at the Scottish Game Fair, having worked hard to get their products to market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, dealers Adi, Melissa, JB, Moses, Lucy, Samantha and Ian bid against one another on a set of mid-century cinema-style seats, Victorian wooden spinning tops, a silver cigarette case, a creepy head-shaped pot and an iconic 1960s movie poster

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack is terrified for Denise and turns his anger on Chelsea for bringing Lucas back to Walford. Ruby tries to persuade Martin into having a romantic evening, but he is more interested in playing computer games than being with her. Gray battles to keep his secrets hidden, while Billy takes a drugs package from Vinny and assures him he knows what he is doing

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The conclusion of a two-part story. Neville must solve a mystery from the past that unlocks the truth of who murdered renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov and why. Help is at hand though, thanks to a former sergeant. Ralf Little stars, with Don Warrington, Josephine Jobert and Sara Martins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney discusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Shakespeare in Love (1998)
programme.desc: The Elizabethan dramatist suffers from writer's block while working on his new play, and to make matters worse creditors are about to close down the theatre. But when a noblewoman disguises herself as a man to fulfil her dream of appearing on stage, the Bard becomes enchanted by her and is inspired to pen the most famous love story of all time. Comedy, with Joseph Fiennes, Gwyneth Paltrow, Judi Dench, Ben Affleck, Geoffrey Rush, Martin Clunes and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Ronnie O'Sullivan and Laila Rouass, while Jose Pizarro and Paul A Young prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek's Premier League action and look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Manchester City

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: Eddie Butler looks ahead to the 2021 Six Nations in the company of former Wales international Sam Warburton, and speaks to Eddie Jones, Serge Betsen, George North and Nigel Owens

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley assists a couple who have £375,000 to spend on a home in the north Dorset countryside, and while in the area, she visits the renowned Lyme Regis Boat Building Academy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a garden in Loughborough with unsafe paving and a cherry tree with troublesome roots

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Joel Dommett and Hannah Cooper Dommett take on Rickie Haywood Williams and Melvin Odoom

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. TV property expert Martin Roberts, author and TV presenter Fern Britton, actress and singer Victoria Ekanoye and TV presenter Arthur Williams answer questions on Thunderbirds, Mary of Teck, Pretty Woman and British Military Aircraft of the Second World War

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as former child stars take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Adrian Hall, Julie Dawn Cole, Lyndyann Barrass, David Peachey, Nick Bond-Owen, Jessica Forte, Sally Thomsett and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha decides to tell Jan she is pregnant, but she needs to find the right moment. The pair are then called to a domestic abuse incident. Lev grapples with the truth that is tearing his family apart, and Leon makes a friend in David when he offers to fix his broken-down car

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DI Lindsay Denton is investigated when she survives the ambush of a police convoy in which three of her colleagues are killed and a witness is seriously injured. AC-12's commanding officer Ted Hastings assigns new recruit Georgia Trotman to work alongside Steve Arnott on the investigation, and suspicions are soon raised about Denton. Starring Adrian Dunbar, Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs in Super Bowl LV at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

programme.title: The Place Beyond the Pines (2012)
programme.desc: A motorcycle stunt rider is reunited with his lost love and the son he has never met. He gives up his life on the road to support them, but ends up drawn into crime, attracting the attention of a troubled, ambitious cop and setting in motion a conflict that will have unforeseen consequences for both men's children. Crime drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Hannah Lemon feels ready for a new and wild adventure away from the London scene, and considers making a move to the Isle of Skye in the Scottish Highlands

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple wanting to uproot from Essex and find a modest cottage in Cornwall to call their forever home. The two nurses have a budget of £340,000

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. First up in the Breed Makeover round are four English cocker spaniels, as the contestants try to tame the gun dogs' wig and furnishings. They are then challenged to create a tricky spiral around the coats of four frizzy-haired models, before parading them on the dogwalk, where their owners see the results for the first time. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Topics include 2D shapes, Standard English and Humanism

programme.title: Teacher Talks
programme.desc: Standard and non-standard English

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga's exploration of the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa concludes. This time, he profiles the three African kings who took a stand against the machinations of an empire, along with a crooner whose voice was considered 'irresistible'. He also investigates a history of race riots in Liverpool, and outlines how the sense of a black British identity has been shaped by various forces throughout the span of the 20th century

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron search for bargains at Ardingly Antiques Fair, where John hopes to get lucky with a wishing well and a painting takes Paul on a Shakespearean journey

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to fish, the most diverse vertebrates on the planet, made up of more than 28,000 species. Off the coast of South Australia, sea dragons pair up for courtship, engaging in a mirror dance before the eggs are laid on the male's tail. Gobies climb up the vertical rocks of Hawaiian waterfalls to access secluded pools at the summit, and flying fish use their elongated pectoral fins to escape from predators. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Punk takes charge of the Zappas and immediately begins planning an attack on the vulnerable Whiskers

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson find some treasures, while Paul Martin hears about pioneering local woman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Much of lockdown has been spent lamenting the loss of attending live performance across sport, music and theatre. Mary finds out why audiences matter and asks what role they play. She asks sports stars whether the loss of a crowd affects their performance, discusses the changing role of the spectator across history with Kwame Kwei-Armah, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Kirsty Sedgman, and attends her first Twitter listening party - a new way for musicians to reach their audience. She also talks to Tim Burgess of the Charlatans, who came up with the idea, and the Rev Richard Coles, former keyboard player with 1980s band the Communards

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson rustles up some of her favourite dishes to share around the table, including a lunch menu featuring herbed leg of lamb served with garlicky roast potatoes with oregano and feta, followed by a soft and rich chocolate olive oil mousse. She also prepares coconut shrimp with turmeric yoghurt, and a ginger and walnut carrot cake

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Following on from the innovative series Your Home Made Perfect, Angela Scanlon returns with this show that offers the same hi-tech makeover technology to outdoor spaces. First up are Demi and Laura from Hertfordshire, who lead a busy family life, meaning they haven't had time to deal with their lacklustre, L-shaped plot. Garden designers Helen Elks-Smith and Manoj Malde pitch for the chance to redesign the site - demonstrating what they would do with virtual reality presentations that put the couple right at the heart of their new, improved outdoor havens. But which one will they choose?

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: A selection of the best moments from the series. Sandi Toksvig asks questions connected to the letter R, as Alan Davies and the guest panellists try to come up with interesting, point-worthy answers - all the while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Glenn Moore and Ahir Shah join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to 1989, while an unlikely duet between a 1960s legend and an 1980s icon proved a chart-topper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve recalls some of the most dangerous experiences of his travels, from coming under fire in war-torn Mogadishu to squaring off with a female wrestler in Mexico City. Among the past destinations featured are a police cell in the far east of Russia, and the programme also looks back at his encounters with ambulance crews in San Salvador and anti-smuggling police in Italy

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, catching up on the drama showing up on the live cameras at his badger sett and the spawning salmon in the River Ness. Chris also takes a look at the reasons why waders bob. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Elliotts are part of Belfast's showbiz scene but Pauline wants a quieter life, and hopes the Causeway Coast will convince husband Robin the time has come for a quieter life

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Northampton find a new home in Norfolk with spectacular views. He also helps a local chef to prepare some seasonal shellfish caught on the coast

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Topics include gravity, forces and music improvisation

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and film-maker Noel Clarke starts his search in Trinidad as he uncovers his ancestry, but soon ends up on one of the smallest and most beautiful islands in the Caribbean. There he learns of an ancestor called Glasgow Bedeau who was born into slavery, and how the music Glasgow's enslaved parents and grandparents passed down reveals the part of Africa from which his ancestors were taken

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron face off at their final battle - the showdown - and with half their items going under the hammer in Brighton, it's anyone's game. Paul unearths a genuine Roman pot, and John tries to carve out a profit with a totem pole, but there can be only one winner

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to birds, revealing the role of their feathers, the ways in which they attract partners and the challenges they face during the nesting season. Narrator David Attenborough provides an insight into the courtship spectacle of the lesser flamingo on Kenya's Lake Bogoria, and follows chinstrap penguins in Antarctica, which make an exhausting climb up a volcano to feed their young

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: New leader Rocket Dog faces a tough challenge as rival group the Commandos invades the Whiskers' territory

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Powderham Castle in Devon, which dating back to the 14th century, is the long-standing home of the Courtenay family. Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Mark Stacey find great treasures among hundreds of visitors to the stately home. Claire meets a steam train fan with a great collection of memorabilia, and Mark gets a surprise when an art deco Spanish bracelet goes under the hammer. Paul also delves into the history of the Met Office

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in a bid to win a jackpot of £3,000

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef explores Burgundy and the Auvergne, sampling a sorrel soup made by a chateau owner from the produce of the garden and showcasing Salers beef and Cantal cheese. Back in his Provencal country kitchen, Rick prepares Cantal bourriols - buckwheat pancakes made with farmhouse eggs and Cep mushrooms

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The keen horticulturalist visits some of the most famous and interesting gardens in the US. He begins his trek in a prairie, the original American flowering wilderness, only to find much of it has disappeared. In New York, he explores vegetables being grown on the city's rooftops, visits an enthusiastic allotment community in the Bronx and learns about the history of America's most famous public green space, Central Park. In Philadelphia, Monty examines one of the country's most famous gardens and enjoys a modern version of a prairie garden

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: Even with the tremendous finds of the divers, Jakob admits that there is still too little evidence to charge the suspect with murder, despite his story having changed dramatically from his initial account. Nikolaj meets with an expert on submarines who contradicts the suspect's latest statement and a forensic computer analysist discovers some very disturbing content on his devices. Soren Malling stars in this drama based on a true story. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: The team members remain focused on being able to establish the guilt of the accused as Jakob reminds them that the benefit of doubt lies with the accused. Jens continues to put pressure on the forensics team to provide answers. but it is Maibritt who finally pieces together enough clues to break the case. Soren Malling and Laura Christensen star in the last part of this drama, based on a true story. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton goes on a caving expedition under the summit of Blaven

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A secret tribunal of former insurgents meets in the bowels of the Galactica to decide the fate of the Cylon collaborators. Chief Tyrol kills a colleague without Admiral Adama's knowledge, while President Zarek authorises the execution of Felix Gaeta. On board a Cylon baseship, Gaius Baltar finds his fate in the hands of an unsympathetic jury, and it falls to Number Six to make a difficult decision

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Gaius struggles with life as a prisoner of the Cylons, who he believes are only keeping him alive in the hope he will lead them to a new home on Earth. Meanwhile, Gaeta locates a nebula using clues from the captive's abandoned research notes - prompting Roslin to order the team to explore it in a bid to find the fabled planet. Sci-fi drama, starring James Callis

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: After a leading trainer shot and badly wounded, a former boxer executed in broad daylight, and a gangland killing at a weigh-in for a European title fight, there is evidence that organised crime is involved in professional boxing. Darragh MacIntyre investigates the murky world behind the glamour of the big fights and the suspected gangster who has been welcomed to boxing's top table. Daniel Kinahan has been named in the courts as the head of one of Europe's biggest drug cartels. So how did he become involved in setting up the fight between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua to become the undisputed heavyweight champion of the world?

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Donald Trump's last months as US president as he attempted to win, and subsequently overturn, the results of the 2020 election - culminating in scenes of violent 'insurrection' against the US Congress. Friends, advisers and close observers tell the story of Trump's last days, from packed rallies - held as coronavirus besieged America - to riots on Capitol Hill. The film provides a blow-by-blow account of the final weeks of his administration - and a gripping psychological study of the controversial leader's final days in power

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Two friends transport their parents back 500 years, with a medieval assault course, a spooky ghost and a Taylor Swift classic given the Tudor treatment

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield and Joe Tracini look back at some of the most impressive dens constructed in the show's first two series, getting feedback from their proud new owners

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: The young cook and his dad prepare one of their favourite savoury snacks - chunky cauliflower nuggets

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team show how to make a lucky drum to mark Lunar New Year

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers settle into new territory, but their neighbours are not friendly and the burrow is in danger. Meanwhile, Mozart and Kinkajou compete for the role of dominant female in the Starsky group. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Womanising male Houdini is on the prowl - and female Flower is ready for breeding. But it remains to be seen whether the two hit it off. Pregnant Kinkajoo emerges as the group's dominant female and Mozart helps Carlos when he begins to suffer from an infected wound. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Big Cats About the House
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Giles Clark at his sanctuary for big cats in Kent. Maya the jaguar is 10 weeks old and is starting to push the family to their limits as she wreaks havoc in the house, destroying the bookshelf, getting into the fridge and ripping up the sofa. However, after an appearance on breakfast TV with Giles, she becomes a big-cat ambassador. Giles also takes in a five-month-old cheetah called Willow, who is recovering from an incident where she broke her paw, and travels to Kenya to help lions in need

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: The first set of quarter-finalists take on a traditional dog groom when they gently removing the dead hairs from the coat of one of the UK's oldest breeds, the Welsh terrier, before producing a very modern look for the Dogwalk that requires smiley faces, flared legs and little outfits. Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don takes his global tour to the US to investigate whether the world's most powerful nation expresses its wealth through its beauty spots. In New York he meets a group of garden guerrillas transforming derelict land into community oases. In Virginia, he visits a conservation project that details America's history, and in California he searches for plots that reflect the glamour of Hollywood

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry challenges the celebrity cooks to prepare the perfect breakfast for houseguests. Two of the contestants adopt a Mexican theme, pitting quesadillas against breakfast burritos, while another presents the judges with an edible crossword. Mary and fellow judges Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin each select their favourite dish, but even this is no protection against being eliminated from the contest. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look through the BBC archive to discover what makes an epic, featuring interviews with some of those who starred in some of the biggest film in the world. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Ivanhoe (1952)
programme.desc: A brave knight defies his father and enlists the help of a rich man and his daughter to rescue Richard the Lionheart from imprisonment. Along the way they also have to counter the machinations of tyrannical Prince John, who is determined their quest will fail. Medieval adventure based on Walter Scott's novel, starring Robert Taylor, Elizabeth Taylor, Joan Fontaine, George Sanders and Guy Rolfe

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Previously unseen finds from the show's travels to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset, Sandon Hall in Staffordshire and Powderham Castle in Devon. Plus, Paul Martin takes a look at the treasures and lavish interiors at Parham House in West Sussex, and finds out how the house was restored to its former Elizabethan glory

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Eight celebrities embark on an experimental adventure to India, to test whether they can set up a more rewarding retirement than in the UK. Entertainer Lionel Blair, wildlife presenter Bill Oddie, actress Amanda Barrie, former world snooker champion Dennis Taylor, TV personality Rustie Lee, doctor and author Miriam Stoppard, singer Sheila Ferguson and actor and singer Paul Nicholas take over a 16th-century mansion in the heart of Old Kochi. Sheila, used to living on her own, is keen to make sure she gets what she wants out of the experience, but Lionel finds dealing with the realities of living in India unsettling

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts the interior design challenge, in which 10 aspiring designers compete to redesign an Oxfordshire show home. Michelle Ogundehin assesses their efforts, and is joined in the first episode by guest judge Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: The lands on the northern edges of the Atlantic bloom as nearly 24 hours of daylight transforms the snow-clad landscapes. Arctic fox clans struggle to raise their cubs on Iceland's volcanic shores, while on Shetland an otter cub strikes out alone, and a lone puffin searches for a partner. Ewan McGregor narrates

programme.title: Perry and Croft: Made in Britain
programme.desc: David Croft and Jimmy Perry collaborated on sitcoms Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum, Hi-de-Hi! and You Rang, M'Lord?, and this documentary is a celebration of how their work can be viewed as a unique chronicle of the UK in the 20th century. The first episode looks at how they depicted the British at war

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring puts the defence of the realm before personal pride, resulting in him nearly losing command of the platoon. Classic comedy with the Home Guard, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Western, starring Armie Hammer, Johnny Depp and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: In Fabric (2018)
programme.desc: A lonely divorcee visits a London department store to find a dress. However, during a busy winter sales period her ideal, yet cursed red gown, passes from person to person, with devastating consequences. Writer-director Peter Strickland's cult comedy horror, starring Sidse Babett Knudsen, Marianne Jean-Baptiste and Julian Barratt

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical compilation, featuring Tina Turner and Phil Collins, Soul II Soul, Paula Abdul, Lisa Stansfield and the Beautiful South. Madchester favourites Stone Roses and Happy Mondays put in an appearance, along with Stock Aitken Waterman stalwarts Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan and Sonia. Plus, Sydney Youngblood, Mike and the Mechanics, Rebel MC and Double Trouble, along with duets from Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Linda Ronstadt and Aaron Neville

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Miranda Krestovnikoff visits Loch Creran on the west coast of Scotland, where she learns about a network of tunnels created by worms. The habitat is thought to be one of the biggest of its kind in the world, and Miranda sets out to discover why more colourful creatures have moved in to share the home with its original builders

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah navigates Snowdonia and the Llanberis Pass, before starting his journey from Bolton to Buckingham Palace to collect his MBE

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a narrow and winding creek flowing slowly through a rocky meadow forest

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James Herriot visits Yorkshire seeking work as a vet with Siegfried Farnon's practice, but his interview becomes a baptism of fire when goes out on the rounds in the Dales countryside. The first episode of the classic BBC drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: Archive interview from 1994 in which Billy Connolly talks to Jeremy Isaacs about stand-up comedy, bodily functions and the night someone set fire to his hair

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri visit Brighton Museum to investigate who painted two religious pictures, and uncover the story of an extravagant building

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Seydou Kane crafts a new kora from scratch in his studio in downtown Dakar, Senegal, an instrument also known as a West African lute or harp that dates back to the 13th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a narrow and winding creek flowing slowly through a rocky meadow forest

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah navigates Snowdonia and the Llanberis Pass, before starting his journey from Bolton to Buckingham Palace to collect his MBE

programme.title: The Great Mountain Sheep Gather
programme.desc: Documentary following a shepherd's journey from summit to valley as he leads his sheep off Scafell Pike, reflecting on life caring for his flock in this rugged landscape. Maxine Peake reads specially commissioned poetry written by Mark Pajak to provide a counterpoint to the shepherd's insights throughout the film.

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The writer and composer looks at the rise of the all-conquering contemporary blockbuster, from Evita to Les Miserables and The Lion King. He also sees how these mega-musicals have met with unexpected competition from quirky hits such as The Rocky Horror Show and Avenue Q. Contributors include Tim Rice, Alan Menkin and Robert Lopez

programme.title: Abba at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of memorable performances by the group. Agnetha Faltskog, Bjorn Ulvaeus, Benny Andersson and Anni-Frid Lyngstad were thrust into the limelight in 1974 when Waterloo triumphed at the Eurovision Song Contest in Brighton, marking the start of their rise to fame. Various outings were captured by BBC cameras over the next few years and several are featured here alongside their Top of the Pops debut, archive interviews and footage featuring hits including Waterloo, Dancing Queen, Does Your Mother Know, Thank You for the Music, SOS, Fernando and Chiquitita. There is also a chance to see the foursome on a 1982 edition of Noel Edmonds' Late, Late Breakfast Show, which was Abba's last televised appearance in the UK

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on August 2, with performances by Little Angels, New Kids on the Block, LFO, Bell Biv Devoe, Madonna, Prince, Dream Warriors, Duran Duran, Partners in Kryme and Tricky Disco

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on August 9 with performances by Bombalurina, DNA featuring Suzanne Vega, Blue Pearl, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, the Soup Dragons featuring Junior Reid, MC Hammer, Tricky Disco, Hothouse Flowers, Partners in Kryme and Together

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at the track Rock Box, reflecting on how Run-DMC's song tore down the barriers between rock and hip-hop, race and class on American radio and television

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at Elevators, by Outkast, which saw hip-hop's southern voice break through with an unsuspecting song that redefined rap's cultural and geographic boundaries

programme.title: Britain's Most Dangerous Songs: Listen to the Banned
programme.desc: The stories of 10 songs, dating from the 1930s to the present day, that were considered unsuitable by the BBC for broadcasting on its radio and television stations. Tunes include My Little Stick of Blackpool Rock by George Formby to the Sex Pistols' God Save the Queen, though it is perhaps surprising that Bing Crosby and the Munchkins are among the other performers on the list. The reasons why these records were censored reveals much of the changing controversies around youth culture. Contributors include Carrie Grant, Paul Morley, Stuart Maconie, Glen Matlock, Mike Read and Jon Robb

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music promoter John Giddings tells the story of how live performance has become a billion-pound industry. Where once bands would tour just to promote an album, in the age of downloads and dwindling record sales, the live arena is booming. The programme also tells the story of the original Isle of Wight festival, where a bunch of young promoters with big ideas persuaded Bob Dylan, the Who and Leonard Cohen to perform. The event transformed the music industry, and, for the young John who was in the audience, inspired a whole career. Contributors include Phil Collins, Stewart Copeland, Mel C and Alex James

programme.title: Abba at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of memorable performances by the group. Agnetha Faltskog, Bjorn Ulvaeus, Benny Andersson and Anni-Frid Lyngstad were thrust into the limelight in 1974 when Waterloo triumphed at the Eurovision Song Contest in Brighton, marking the start of their rise to fame. Various outings were captured by BBC cameras over the next few years and several are featured here alongside their Top of the Pops debut, archive interviews and footage featuring hits including Waterloo, Dancing Queen, Does Your Mother Know, Thank You for the Music, SOS, Fernando and Chiquitita. There is also a chance to see the foursome on a 1982 edition of Noel Edmonds' Late, Late Breakfast Show, which was Abba's last televised appearance in the UK

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The writer and composer looks at the rise of the all-conquering contemporary blockbuster, from Evita to Les Miserables and The Lion King. He also sees how these mega-musicals have met with unexpected competition from quirky hits such as The Rocky Horror Show and Avenue Q. Contributors include Tim Rice, Alan Menkin and Robert Lopez

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The adventurer continues his journey, travelling from K2 in Pakistan to Ladakh in India, a relatively short distance geographically, but one that will take a considerable feat of logistics owing to the region's politics. He passes through the Sikh city of Amritsar, spends time on a houseboat on Lake Dal, discovers his previous incarnations at the Astrological Institute, then travels on to an appointment with the Dalai Lama

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a former prostitute, whose sister reveals that she had recently fallen in love, leading the police to her meek and gentle boyfriend. In italian

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are celebrating another noisy victory, gleefully ringing Big Ben's bell when an over-excited Penfold falls, pushing the clock hand forward, causing the sun to go down prematurely. Unbeknownst to them a mysterious figure watches on from nearby noting the clock's incredible time-controlling powers. When DM realises that the day is being stolen and replaced with a silent night, he enlists the help of expert colleagues - the night shift! With Danger Moth and Danger Mole in tow the team tracks the quiet culprit - but a noisy Danger Mouse might just be outwitted by this stealthy saboteur

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she discovers that astronauts live in space on a spacecraft called the International Space Station and that there have been different astronauts living there for over 20 years. Maddie shows us how astronauts live and work on the International Space Station and do lots of different jobs and research in order to learn more about space and our planet Earth. We explore a laboratory where they do experiments, the food they eat, how they wash and even how they go to the toilet. We find out that the space station is made from thousands of parts that were put together by astronauts in space, and we learn that it’s also a home because it’s a place where astronauts eat, sleep and have fun.

Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov is shot dead during an apparent burglary, Neville is quick to conclude that the scene was staged and Pasha was murdered. But the four people under suspicion - the victim's family and domestic workers - all have seemingly solid alibis. However, matters are complicated when the discovery of new evidence links the crime to a case from 20 years ago. First of a two-part story, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: Question Time

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by heading to Thailand, where they discover why Bangkok has a reputation as the street food capital of the world, and help serve up school dinners. programme.desc: Fabio and Isobel Avemaria think living by the sea is just what their young family needs after health scares and redundancy. They consider whether now is the right time to make a move to Norfolk. Presented by Chris Bavin

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas take an entertaining look at Wales' fortunes at this year's Six Nations. They are joined by one of the world's most sought-after opera stars, Wales' very own Bryn Terfel, and former scrum-half Gareth Edwards, while Irishman Ed Byrne provides the laughs. Plus, the four regions of Wales battle it out in a series of challenges to see who can be crowned champions of the Sin Bin Showdown

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack is terrified for Denise and turns his anger on Chelsea for bringing Lucas back to Walford. Gray battles to keep his secrets hidden, while Billy takes a drugs package from Vinny and assures him he knows what he is doing

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The conclusion of a two-part story. Neville must solve a mystery from the past that unlocks the truth of who murdered renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov and why. Help is at hand though, thanks to a former sergeant. Ralf Little stars, with Don Warrington, Josephine Jobert and Sara Martins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney discusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Shakespeare in Love (1998)
programme.desc: The Elizabethan dramatist suffers from writer's block while working on his new play, and to make matters worse creditors are about to close down the theatre. But when a noblewoman disguises herself as a man to fulfil her dream of appearing on stage, the Bard becomes enchanted by her and is inspired to pen the most famous love story of all time. Comedy, with Joseph Fiennes, Gwyneth Paltrow, Judi Dench, Ben Affleck, Geoffrey Rush, Martin Clunes and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Ronnie O'Sullivan and Laila Rouass, while Jose Pizarro and Paul A Young prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.desc: Ginny Buckley assists a couple who have £375,000 to spend on a home in the north Dorset countryside, and while in the area, she visits the renowned Lyme Regis Boat Building Academy

programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: The first set of quarter-finalists take on a traditional dog groom when they gently removing the dead hairs from the coat of one of the UK's oldest breeds, the Welsh terrier, before producing a very modern look for the Dogwalk that requires smiley faces, flared legs and little outfits. Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov is shot dead during an apparent burglary, Neville is quick to conclude that the scene was staged and Pasha was murdered. But the four people under suspicion - the victim's family and domestic workers - all have seemingly solid alibis. However, matters are complicated when the discovery of new evidence links the crime to a case from 20 years ago. First of a two-part story, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Slipping further away from Poppy, Stevie struggles to hold his temper when Cameron antagonises him. Ruby's loft clear-out reveals something Caitlin would rather have stayed hidden, and Lenny learns the dreadful truth of why Amber is in Shieldinch

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The clan's visit to the Second World War graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start when Lucy discovers that her grandfather was not the man she thought he was. In short, this seems to be a piece of family history best left buried. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack is terrified for Denise and turns his anger on Chelsea for bringing Lucas back to Walford. Ruby tries to persuade Martin into having a romantic evening, but he is more interested in playing computer games than being with her. Gray battles to keep his secrets hidden, while Billy takes a drugs package from Vinny and assures him he knows what he is doing

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The conclusion of a two-part story. Neville must solve a mystery from the past that unlocks the truth of who murdered renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov and why. Help is at hand though, thanks to a former sergeant. Ralf Little stars, with Don Warrington, Josephine Jobert and Sara Martins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney discusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan, Craig Fowler and Robert Borthwick present a fun-filled look at Scottish football. Regular features include Future Headlines, in which the team members make predictions on what will happen this weekend based upon imagined newspaper headlines, Twitter hot-takes and forum meltdowns

programme.title: Shakespeare in Love (1998)
programme.desc: The Elizabethan dramatist suffers from writer's block while working on his new play, and to make matters worse creditors are about to close down the theatre. But when a noblewoman disguises herself as a man to fulfil her dream of appearing on stage, the Bard becomes enchanted by her and is inspired to pen the most famous love story of all time. Comedy, with Joseph Fiennes, Gwyneth Paltrow, Judi Dench, Ben Affleck, Geoffrey Rush, Martin Clunes and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Ronnie O'Sullivan and Laila Rouass, while Jose Pizarro and Paul A Young prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek's Premier League action and look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Manchester City

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: Eddie Butler looks ahead to the 2021 Six Nations in the company of former Wales international Sam Warburton, and speaks to Eddie Jones, Serge Betsen, George North and Nigel Owens

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley assists a couple who have £375,000 to spend on a home in the north Dorset countryside, and while in the area, she visits the renowned Lyme Regis Boat Building Academy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a garden in Loughborough with unsafe paving and a cherry tree with troublesome roots

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson to present the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Joel Dommett and Hannah Cooper Dommett take on Rickie Haywood Williams and Melvin Odoom

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. TV property expert Martin Roberts, author and TV presenter Fern Britton, actress and singer Victoria Ekanoye and TV presenter Arthur Williams answer questions on Thunderbirds, Mary of Teck, Pretty Woman and British Military Aircraft of the Second World War

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as former child stars take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Adrian Hall, Julie Dawn Cole, Lyndyann Barrass, David Peachey, Nick Bond-Owen, Jessica Forte, Sally Thomsett and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha decides to tell Jan she is pregnant, but she needs to find the right moment. The pair are then called to a domestic abuse incident. Lev grapples with the truth that is tearing his family apart, and Leon makes a friend in David when he offers to fix his broken-down car

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DI Lindsay Denton is investigated when she survives the ambush of a police convoy in which three of her colleagues are killed and a witness is seriously injured. AC-12's commanding officer Ted Hastings assigns new recruit Georgia Trotman to work alongside Steve Arnott on the investigation, and suspicions are soon raised about Denton. Starring Adrian Dunbar, Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Celtic v Motherwell, Hibernian v Aberdeen and Livingston v St Johnstone

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs in Super Bowl LV at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

AC-12's commanding officer Ted Hastings assigns new recruit Georgia Trotman to work alongside Steve Arnott on the investigation, and suspicions are soon raised about Denton. Starring Adrian Dunbar, Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs in Super Bowl LV at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

programme.title: The Place Beyond the Pines (2012)
programme.desc: A motorcycle stunt rider is reunited with his lost love and the son he has never met. He gives up his life on the road to support them, but ends up drawn into crime, attracting the attention of a troubled, ambitious cop and setting in motion a conflict that will have unforeseen consequences for both men's children. Crime drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the head of NHS Test and Trace, Baroness Harding, as well as Mark Hewlett, Director of Testing at NHS Test and Trace and Steve McManus, Director of Tracing at NHS Test and Trace, from Wednesday 3 February.

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special edition of Witness History, Larry Madowo brings us five extraordinary stories to mark Black History Month from Washington DC. We hear about one of the darkest days in the civil rights era, when a baptist church was bombed, killing four young black girls, we spend some time at the Dance Theatre of Harlem - the first dance company in America to prioritise black dancers - and we remember the damning 1960s report that told the US it was fundamentally racist.

programme.desc: Is the Government's plan to instantly mass test for Covid-19 proving more trouble than it is worth? programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne resigns from her job, and Simon's inwardly gutted, knowing that she now depends on him more than ever. Gail discovers her dad, Ted, has died, and Johnny tells Jenny that his condition has worsened

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The discovery of a 2,000-year-old body provides fresh evidence in a five-year-old unsolved missing persons case, so Morse and Thursday take up the investigation, discovering a possible link to a local pagan ritual to celebrate the harvest. The investigation leads them to a nuclear power station, where they face a conspiracy of silence concerning an experimental new reactor and a potential disaster that could devastate Oxford. Guest starring Sheila Hancock, widow of original Inspector Morse star John Thaw

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Presented by Jeremy Clarkson

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the guessing game singing contest, with seven mystery singers taking to the stage tonight, only for two of them to be eliminated and unmasked. Joel is joined by panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Mo Gilligan, plus special guest Alan Carr

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Entertainment news

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole's former mentor from his CIA years Tom Barnes appears with some alarming news, as he and Murtaugh set about investigating the robbery of safe deposit boxes, whose suspects seem connected to his past. Meanwhile, Murtaugh encounters his wife's new client Louie Gutierrez. 'The Gute', as she is colloquially known, immediately proves to be a strong adversary, causing upset with every move she makes, prompting him to enlist Bailey's help to investigate her. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney

programme.desc: Anna is practising for the school cycling test and is good at it. Dave isn't quite as skilled, but with a new bike from his parents he really hopes to pass.

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry are hired by a British super spy to help guard the Queen's precious objects while they are on display in the city

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
